target.title: fix(agents): strip truncation sentinels from final replies
target.body:
## Summary

This PR prevents internal truncation sentinel lines from leaking into final assistant-visible text.

- Strips standalone OpenClaw/Codex truncation markers such as `...(truncated)...`, `...(live output truncated)...`, `[... 42 more characters truncated]`, and bootstrap read sentinels from delivery text.
- Preserves ordinary prose, inline mentions, and fenced-code examples of those markers, including longer Markdown fences that contain triple-backtick examples.
- Keeps `chat.history` display truncation markers visible for history-derived session reads, so nested/session callers do not mistake a shortened history preview for a complete reply.
- Intentionally leaves producer-side truncation behavior unchanged; this only hardens the final sanitizer/history extraction boundary.

AI-assisted: yes. Codex was used locally to draft and review the patch; markoub is the submitting author and reviewed the resulting diff and validation.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #82121

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #82128

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request; this follows the open issue report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: final assistant-visible sanitizer output no longer includes standalone internal truncation sentinel lines, while history-derived replies preserve the explicit `chat.history` truncation signal.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `markoub/strip-truncation-sentinels`, commit `34d23e86db`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module --eval 'import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.ts";
const samples = [
  ["mixed final text", ["Visible before.", "", "[... 42 more characters truncated]", "...(truncated)...", "Visible after."].join("\n")],
  ["bootstrap read sentinel", "[...truncated, read AGENTS.md for full content...]"],
  ["codex middle marker line", "…9 tokens truncated…"],
  ["inline prose", "The literal marker is `...(truncated)...`."],
  ["fenced example", ["Example:", "```", "...(truncated)...", "```"].join("\n")],
  ["long fence example", ["````md", "```", "[... 42 more characters truncated]", "```", "````"].join("\n")],
  ["history marker default", ["Partial reply", "...(truncated)..."].join("\n")],
  ["history marker preserved", ["Partial reply", "...(truncated)..."].join("\n"), { preserveDisplayTruncationMarkers: true }],
];
for (const [name, input, opts] of samples) {
  console.log(`${name}: ${JSON.stringify(sanitizeUserFacingText(input, opts))}`);
}'
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
mixed final text: "Visible before.\n\nVisible after."
bootstrap read sentinel: ""
codex middle marker line: ""
inline prose: "The literal marker is `...(truncated)...`."
fenced example: "Example:\n```\n...(truncated)...\n```"
long fence example: "````md\n```\n[... 42 more characters truncated]\n```\n````"
history marker default: "Partial reply\n"
history marker preserved: "Partial reply\n...(truncated)..."
```

- Observed result after fix: standalone delivery sentinels are removed; prose/code examples remain intact; the history opt-in keeps the display truncation marker visible.
- What was not tested: a live provider turn through a real external LLM and a browser UI screenshot.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` normally delegates to Blacksmith, but this local machine is not authenticated with `blacksmith auth login`; I ran the equivalent local child lane with `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1` and it passed.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): issue #82121 reports leaked `...(truncated)...` / `[... N more characters truncated]` sentinels appearing in final assistant replies.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts src/agents/run-wait.test.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.agents.json --noEmit
git diff --check
PATH="$HOME/.local/node_modules/corepack/shims:$HOME/.local/node_modules/.bin:$PATH" OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed
codex review --base origin/main
```

Results:

- Focused Vitest passed: `140` sanitizer tests and `25` run-wait tests.
- Agent test typecheck passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Local `pnpm check:changed` child lane passed for `core, coreTests`.
- `codex review --base origin/main` final run reported: `No prioritized, actionable correctness issues were found in the changed sanitizer and chat-history extraction paths.`

Regression coverage added:

- Stripping standalone truncation sentinel lines.
- Preserving ordinary inline/prose examples.
- Preserving sentinel examples inside fenced code and longer Markdown fences.
- Preserving `chat.history` truncation markers for latest assistant reply extraction.

Known failing broad command:

```bash
PATH="$HOME/.local/node_modules/corepack/shims:$HOME/.local/node_modules/.bin:$PATH" CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test:changed
```

This failed in an unrelated existing unit-fast shard:

```text
src/agents/memory-search.test.ts > memory search config > accepts Gemini multimodal memory even when the runtime registry has not registered Gemini yet
Error: [vitest] No "getActivePluginRegistryWorkspaceDir" export is defined on the "../plugins/runtime.js" mock.
```

The failure is outside the touched files and references a plugin runtime mock export, not the sanitizer/history paths changed here.

Codex-related source inspection performed:

- `../codex/codex-rs/utils/string/src/truncate.rs:131-136` for `…N tokens/chars truncated…` marker formatting.
- `../codex/codex-rs/utils/output-truncation/src/lib.rs:12-25` for tool/output truncation helpers.
- `../codex/codex-rs/core/src/tools/context.rs:404-407` for exec output truncation into model-visible text.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

False positives in final text sanitization, especially user-provided examples that mention truncation markers.

How is that risk mitigated?

The stripper only removes whole sentinel-looking lines, skips Markdown fenced-code spans using the existing `markdown-core` fence parser, preserves inline/prose mentions, and preserves the exact `chat.history` display marker only in the history extraction path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. I have not run a live external LLM turn.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local `codex review --base origin/main` initially found two issues before this PR was opened: longer Markdown fences and `chat.history` marker preservation. Both were fixed and the final local Codex review reported no prioritized actionable issues.
